crates.name: ensi-core
crates.responsibility: Domain entities and port traits (hexagonal architecture inner layer). No I/O, no frameworks.

crates.name: ensi-db
crates.responsibility: SurrealDB 3.0 embedded adapter. Implements all *Repository traits from ensi-core.

crates.name: ensi-events
crates.responsibility: NATS JetStream embedded adapter. Implements EventPublisher trait. Defines subject hierarchy.

crates.name: ensi-pipeline
crates.responsibility: Pipeline FSM: state transitions, guards, artifact validation. Calls AgentExecutor via trait.

crates.name: ensi-server
crates.responsibility: Axum 0.8 HTTP server: REST API, WebSocket hub, static file serving (Leptos SSR). JWT/PAT auth middleware.

crates.name: ensi-daemon
crates.responsibility: Task poller/subscriber. Execenv lifecycle (~/ensi_workspaces/{ws_id}/{task_id}/). Hosts AgentExecutor impl (Gemini API calls, schema preprocessing, retry loop).

crates.name: ensi-frontend
crates.responsibility: Leptos SSR + WASM client. Click-to-move issue board, pipeline view, issue detail, agent task feed.

crates.name: ensi-cli
crates.responsibility: CLI commands for workspace management, issue CRUD, pipeline control, and PAT management.

transitions.guard: CodeReviewArtifact schema-valid and approved flag true
human sets blocked_stage=Discovery and resets retry_count
human sets blocked_stage=Planning and resets retry_count
human sets blocked_stage=Architecture and resets retry_count
human sets blocked_stage=Development and resets retry_count
human sets blocked_stage=Review and resets retry_count

retry_policy.error_injection_template: Your previous response violated the output schema. Error: {validation_error}. Retry with a corrected response.
retry_policy.note: Retry loop is encapsulated in AgentExecutor.execute(). FSM only sees success or max_retries_exceeded.

agent_executor.trait_definition: AgentExecutor: model_for_stage(stage: PipelineStage) -> GeminiModel, execute(stage, context, schema) -> Result<Value, ExecutorError>
agent_executor.model_selection_note: gemini-2.0-flash deprecated for new accounts (404 on first run). Updated to gemini-2.5-flash. gemini-2.5-pro unchanged.
agent_executor.max_output_tokens_note: 4096 caused truncation on PrdArtifact with gemini-2.5-pro (~192-line responses). 8192 required for Pro stages.

latency_validated.retry_loop_note: responseSchema constraint overrides contradictory prompt instructions — compliance was 100% without retries. Retry loop remains as safety net for edge cases.
latency_validated.nfr_status: PASS — p95 Pro ~50s per call; 2 Pro calls (PRD+Arch) + 3 Flash calls (Brief+Stories+Review) = ~115s typical pipeline. Well within 5-min NFR.

naming_conventions.timestamps: created_at + updated_at on all mutable tables
naming_conventions.soft_deletes: none in v1 — hard delete only

api_surface.versioning: URL path prefix — no header-based versioning

rest_endpoints.description: List issues (filters: status, priority, assignee, limit, offset)
Update issue (title, description, status, priority, assignee)

v1_uis: Pipeline View (5 stages with status indicators)
Agent task feed (live updates via WebSocket)
deferred_to_v1_1: Drag-and-drop board (DnD library not available in Leptos v1)

patterns.state_management: Leptos signals and resources (no external state lib)
patterns.websocket: leptos WebSocket resource polling task feed
patterns.ssr: Axum-hosted Leptos SSR, hydrated on client

known_constraints: borrow-checker + clone-before-capture pattern required in closures
LOC ~30% larger than equivalent Next.js — budget accordingly
3-4 day learning curve for Rust devs new to reactive UI

daemon.task_lifecycle: poll NATS ensi.task.created → create execenv → inject context files → spawn claude-code subprocess → stream messages via NATS → on exit: submit artifact or report failure

context_files.content: Current pipeline state, previous artifact summaries

daemon.output_streaming: task_message rows + NATS publish on each line
daemon.cleanup: execenv deleted after task completion (success or failure after max retries)

mechanisms.usage: Long-lived API access for members. Stored as bcrypt hash in pat table.
mechanisms.claims: same as JWT but loaded from DB on each request

mechanisms.usage: Per-task short-lived token (exp: task TTL + 1h). Only allows daemon endpoints.

auth.enforcement: Axum middleware layer — extracts token from Authorization: Bearer <token>, resolves identity, attaches to request extensions
auth.authorization: Workspace-scoped RBAC: owner > admin > member. Agent tokens are workspace-scoped with pipeline-only permissions.

adr.title: Leptos SSR + WASM over Next.js / SvelteKit
adr.context: Frontend framework selection for Ensi v1. Team is Rust-first. SP-02 validated Leptos feasibility.
adr.decision: Leptos 0.7 with Axum SSR and WASM hydration.
consequences: POSITIVE: Single language (Rust) across full stack — no context switch
POSITIVE: Single binary deployment — Axum serves both API and SSR frontend
POSITIVE: Type-safe server functions — no REST/GraphQL layer for UI calls
NEGATIVE: DnD not available — deferred to v1.1 (click-to-move instead)
NEGATIVE: LOC ~30% larger than Next.js equivalent
NEGATIVE: 3-4 day learning curve for devs new to Leptos reactive model
ALTERNATIVE REJECTED: Next.js — adds Node.js runtime, breaks single-binary deploy, splits language stack

adr.title: Gemini model selection by pipeline stage (Flash vs Pro)
adr.context: SP-01 validated Gemini structured output. Cost/latency tradeoff between Flash and Pro varies by stage complexity.
adr.decision: gemini-2.5-flash for Discovery, Development, Review (simpler structured outputs). gemini-2.5-pro for Planning and Architecture (complex reasoning and large schemas). NOTE: gemini-2.0-flash deprecated for new accounts — replaced by gemini-2.5-flash per T-008 real validation.
consequences: POSITIVE: Flash reduces cost and latency for high-frequency stages
POSITIVE: Pro quality for the two most architecturally critical stages
NEGATIVE: Two SDK configurations to maintain
ALTERNATIVE REJECTED: Pro-only — 3-4x cost increase, latency acceptable but unnecessary for simpler tasks
ALTERNATIVE REJECTED: Flash-only — SP-01 showed quality degradation on complex PRD/Architecture schemas

adr.title: AgentExecutor retry policy: error injection over re-generation
adr.context: T-008 real validation: gemini-2.5-flash achieved 10/10 compliance and gemini-2.5-pro achieved 5/5 compliance when responseSchema is set. Retry was not triggered — responseSchema overrides contradictory prompts. Retry loop kept as safety net for production edge cases.
adr.decision: Maximum 3 retries with error injection: on validation failure, the validation error is appended to the prompt as context for the next attempt. Retry loop is owned by AgentExecutor, not Pipeline FSM.
consequences: POSITIVE: 85-90% of violations resolved within 3 attempts per SP-01
POSITIVE: FSM stays simple — only sees success or max_retries_exceeded
POSITIVE: Error injection gives the model context to self-correct without a full re-generation
NEGATIVE: Up to 3 Gemini API calls per stage on bad runs — ~3x latency in worst case
ALTERNATIVE REJECTED: Schema simplification only — reduces but doesn't eliminate failures
ALTERNATIVE REJECTED: Retry without error injection — lower success rate per SP-01

adr.title: SurrealDB 3.0 embedded over PostgreSQL
adr.context: Need a persistent store that supports single-binary deployment and flexible schema for artifact blobs.
adr.decision: SurrealDB 3.0 in embedded mode (no separate DB process). Data stored under ~/ensi_data/.
consequences: POSITIVE: Single binary — no separate DB process to manage
POSITIVE: SCHEMAFULL mode gives schema enforcement without a migration framework
POSITIVE: Native record links replace foreign keys for graph-like relations (issue → project → workspace)
POSITIVE: `object` field type stores artifact JSON blobs natively without serialization overhead
NEGATIVE: Smaller ecosystem than PostgreSQL — fewer ORM options
NEGATIVE: SurrealDB 3.0 is relatively new — fewer production case studies
NEGATIVE: No horizontal scaling in embedded mode — single node only (acceptable for v1 scope)
ALTERNATIVE REJECTED: PostgreSQL + separate process — breaks single-binary deploy and adds ops complexity

adr.title: Click-to-move over drag-and-drop for issue board v1
adr.context: SP-02 confirmed no mature DnD library for Leptos WASM. Implementing DnD from scratch is high-risk.
adr.decision: Click-to-move: clicking an issue opens a column picker dropdown. Drag-and-drop deferred to v1.1.
consequences: POSITIVE: Eliminates high-risk custom DnD implementation
POSITIVE: Accessible by default — keyboard-navigable column picker
POSITIVE: Works identically on desktop and mobile
NEGATIVE: Less intuitive UX than DnD for power users
NEGATIVE: Feature parity gap vs. Jira/Linear until v1.1
ALTERNATIVE REJECTED: Custom DnD from scratch — estimated 2-3 week spike, unacceptable for 8-week MVP

technical_risks.risk: gemini-2.5-pro latency ~43-50s per call — two Pro stages (PRD+Arch) add ~90-100s to pipeline
technical_risks.mitigation: NFR target is 5 min — typical pipeline ~115s total (well within limit). Monitor p95 in production; fallback to Flash if Pro SLA degrades beyond 90s.

technical_risks.risk: SurrealDB 3.0 embedded stability — limited production data
technical_risks.mitigation: Use SCHEMAFULL mode to catch schema violations early. Add DB health check to server startup.

technical_risks.risk: Leptos reactive model learning curve causes schedule slip
technical_risks.mitigation: Reserve 3-4 days frontend onboarding. Start with static Leptos components before adding reactivity.
technical_risks.owner: SM to budget in sprint plan

open_items.description: RESOLVED — T-008 executed spike with real GEMINI_API_KEY on 2026-04-18. Real metrics: Flash 10/10 compliance p50=6361ms p95=7980ms; Pro 5/5 compliance p50=42754ms p95=50285ms. Architecture updated.
Artifact schemas for SprintPlanArtifact and CodeReviewArtifact not yet defined in prd-artifact.json
open_items.action: SM and Dev agents must define these schemas before their respective pipeline stages begin
